# Copilot Instructions â€” asmd-ai-assisted-code

## Project Context

This is an **educational Java lab** for exploring LLM integration via `langchain4j`.  
The build system is **Gradle** (Kotlin DSL); all application and test code is **Java**.

Before generating any code, read:

- [ARCHITECTURE.md](../ARCHITECTURE.md) â€” package map, design principles, LLM patterns
- [PRODUCT.md](../PRODUCT.md) â€” vision, objectives, user personas, success criteria
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](../CONTRIBUTING.md) â€” code style, test conventions, dependency rules

---

## Always-On Rules

### Java
- Interface first, then implementation.
- Constructor injection only; no static state.
- `final` on implementation classes; `private final` on all fields.
- `Objects.requireNonNull` on every constructor parameter.
- No wildcard imports. 4-space indentation.
- Wrap every `ChatModel.chat(...)` in a retry loop; throw `IllegalStateException` after `MAX_RETRIES`.
- Prompt classes implement a `*Prompt` interface with a single `toPromptString()` method.
- Decode LLM JSON via `JsonCodec`; never instantiate `new Gson()` in application code.

### Gradle
- One `dependency` per line.
- Extract shared version numbers as `val`s at the top of `build.gradle.kts`.
- Test-only deps get `testImplementation` or `testRuntimeOnly`. Do not duplicate existing library functionality.

### Testing
- JUnit 5 + Mockito. Pattern: Arrange / Act / Assert (AAA).
- Method names: `should<Expected>When<Condition>`.
- Mock `ChatModel` / `EmbeddingModel`; never call real Ollama or Gemini in unit tests.
- Every test must be independent â€” no shared mutable state.

Use the TDD Agent (`@tdd`) whenever you are asked to implement a new feature, fix a bug, or add tests.

---

## Anti-patterns (never do these)

- Do not call `new Gson()` directly in application code.
- Do not hardcode model names or API keys in production classes.
- Do not write production code before a failing test exists (unless it is a standalone demo in `basics/`).
- Do not add dependencies that duplicate existing functionality (e.g., no Jackson when Gson is present).
- Do not use `import java.util.*` or any other wildcard import.
- Do not share mutable state between test methods.
